mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discounting conditions and a lower cost of capital, which lifts equity multiples and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the policy path is still moving down.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in more than 55% of months where FEDFUNDS has declined over the prior 3 months, or if the signal’s rolling 2-year Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in UNRATE above a short-run threshold marks labor-market deterioration that reduces expected earnings growth and raises recession odds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the average triggered return is not statistically below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS is accelerating while HOUST is weak, consumer spending is outpacing housing-sensitive domestic growth, a pattern that supports near-term revenue visibility and positive SPX returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3m annualized growth > 0 and HOUST 3m change < 0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not improve 5-day forward SPX returns versus baseline, or if either proxy alone explains the effect better than the interaction term in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO combined with firm CPIAUCSL implies supply contraction with persistent inflation pressure, which compresses real margins and raises the probability of negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of falling INDPRO with rising CPIAUCSL do not produce negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not significant after controlling for each series individually.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% indicates restrictive policy that tightens financial conditions and pressures equity duration, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate regimes are not followed by below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if forward returns remain positive in a majority of observations with FEDFUNDS above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in PAYEMS and a stable or falling UNRATE signals broad labor absorption, supporting household income growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3m change > 0 and UNRATE not rising over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor-strength condition fails to outperform unconditional 20-day returns, or if the signal disappears once PAYEMS growth is removed from the joint specification.

mechanism_narrative: A broad deceleration in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies weakening final demand that reduces sales expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint downturns in GDPC1 and RSAFS are not associated with negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect is present when only one of the two demand series weakens.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising HOUST together with rising INDPRO signals improving capacity utilization and easing bottlenecks, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if recoveries in HOUST and INDPRO do not lead to above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the relationship vanishes outside post-shock recovery periods.
